title: RTP Network Adoption & Growth
summary: RTP network showing momentum in adoption. 6 developments tracked spanning 3 categories. Key insight: Real-time settlement eliminates fraud exposure delays inherent in batch-based ACH processing. Competitive positioning: RTP competes directly with card networks (Visa Direct, Mastercard Send) and stablecoins on speed and cost. Tracked by: Finextra, Digital Transactions, Payments Dive.

relatedBriefs.brief: ACH balance validation practices that assume a 120% deposit cushion are insufficient risk mitigation, causing financial institutions to lose deposits when actual fraud exceeds expected thresholds—highlighting that traditional rail safeguards rely on flawed assumptions rather than real-time verification, creating competitive opportunity for real-time rails (RTP, FedNow) that eliminate settlement delays and reduce fraud exposure through immediate fund confirmation.

links.name: Finextra
links.title: The 120% Rule Cost Us Real Deposits — And Taught Us to Stop Borrowing Other People's Risk Assumption

relatedBriefs.brief: The RTP network continues to see strong transaction growth with financial institutions expanding use cases beyond traditional transfers. Recent developments show increased adoption for earned wage access and tax refunds, positioning RTP as a viable alternative to batch-based payment processing for time-sensitive transactions.

links.name: Digital Transactions
links.title: How Cards Are Exploiting Niche Payments Opportunities

relatedBriefs.brief: J.P. Morgan and industry leaders recognize that agentic commerce—autonomous AI-driven transactions—requires robust governance and trust infrastructure rather than advanced AI alone, while SoftBank and OpenAI's AI-powered cybersecurity offering addresses the rising threat of sophisticated attacks targeting payment systems. This shift signals that B2B/B2C payment rails will increasingly compete on risk management and compliance capabilities alongside speed, making security-first infrastructure a key competitive differentiator for traditional rails (ACH, wire, RTP) seeking to support autonomous payment flows.

links.name: PaymentsJournal
links.title: SoftBank and OpenAI Team Up on AI-Fueled Enterprise Cybersecurity

links.name: Tearsheet
links.title: J.P. Morgan Payments’ Michael Lozanoff on why agentic commerce can’t scale without governance

links.title: The Post-Password Era: Rethinking Authentication in Financial Services

relatedBriefs.brief: Real-Time Payments (RTP) network showing continued development. The Future of Same Day ACH, RTP, and Virtual Cards. Strategic implication: RTP adoption accelerates as financial institutions expand use cases and integration capabilities.

links.name: PaymentsJournal
links.title: The Future of Same Day ACH, RTP, and Virtual Cards

relatedBriefs.brief: AI-driven personalization is reshaping consumer payment expectations—users now expect instant, individualized payment experiences similar to other digital services, pressuring card networks and fintech players to move beyond static offerings. This shift favors platforms investing in real-time, context-aware payment customization (RTP, FedNow, and embedded finance solutions) over traditional card-centric models, creating competitive advantage for providers who can deliver hyper-personalized B2C payment journeys at scale.

links.name: PaymentsJournal
links.title: Serving a Segment of One: The Race to Stay Top of Wallet

title: FedNow Service Deployment
summary: FedNow Service (Federal Reserve 24/7 instant payment rail) deployment accelerating. 4 announcements across 3 categories. Strategic impact: Federal infrastructure directly competes with private payment networks. Bank adoption drives standardization and interoperability. Market implications: Regulatory backing gives FedNow distribution advantage vs. proprietary networks. Sources: Finextra, Tearsheet, PaymentsJournal.

relatedBriefs.brief: ACH balance validation practices that assume a 120% deposit cushion are insufficient risk mitigation, causing financial institutions to lose deposits when actual fraud exceeds expected thresholds—highlighting that traditional rail safeguards rely on flawed assumptions rather than real-time verification, creating competitive opportunity for real-time rails (RTP, FedNow) that eliminate settlement delays and reduce fraud exposure through immediate fund confirmation.

links.name: Finextra
links.title: The 120% Rule Cost Us Real Deposits — And Taught Us to Stop Borrowing Other People's Risk Assumption

relatedBriefs.brief: FedNow participation continues expanding with focus on risk mitigation tools. The Federal Reserve has launched new network intelligence capabilities to help participants assess account-level risk in real-time, addressing a key concern for adoption among smaller financial institutions.

links.title: The 3-Min Read: The two systems Coinbase is building for an agent-driven financial ecosystem

title: Stablecoins in US Payment Infrastructure
